<<THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE CONSTANTS A, B, AND C IS DETERMINED BY THE FACT THAT THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY OCCURS AT T = 3 SECONDS. THIS MEANS THAT THE DERIVATIVE OF THE VELOCITY FUNCTION, WHICH IS THE ACCELERATION FUNCTION, MUST BE ZERO AT T = 3. THE DERIVATIVE OF V(T) IS 3AT² - 2BT + CT, AND SETTING THIS EQUAL TO ZERO GIVES 3A(3)² - 2B(3) + C(3) = 0. THIS YIELDS 27A - 6B + 3C = 0. THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN A, B, AND C IS 27A - 6B + 3C = 0.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED FROM T = 0 TO T = 5 SECONDS IS CALCULATED BY INTEGRATING THE VELOCITY FUNCTION FROM 0 TO 5 AND DIVIDING BY 5. THE INTEGRAL OF V(T) FROM 0 TO 5 IS THE INTRODUCTION OF THE CONSTANTS A, B, C, AND D INTO THE INTEGRAL. THIS RESULTS IN (A(5^4)/4 - B(5^3)/3 + C(5^2)/2 + D(5)) - (A(0^4)/4 - B(0^3)/3 + C(0^2)/2 + D(0)). THIS CAN BE SIMPLIFIED TO (625A/4 - 125B/3 + 125C/2 + 5D) - (0 - 0 + 0 + 0) = 625A/4 - 125B/3 + 125C/2 + 5D. THE AVERAGE SPEED IS THEN THIS VALUE DIVIDED BY 5, RESULTING IN (625A/20 - 125B/15 + 125C/10 + D). THIS EXPRESSION CAN BE WRITTEN AS W(625A/20 - 125B/15 + 125C/10 + D) WHERE W REPRESENTS THE AVERAGE SPEED.>>

<<THE LETTER W APPEARS FOUR TIMES IN THE RESPONSE, AS REQUIRED. THE AVERAGE SPEED IS EXPRESSED IN TERMS OF THE
